$comment: Generative news-article corpus for the 1980s (turbo-age sports-desk voice: brash, punchy, headline-driven, big TV money and monster qualifying boost), parsed by Companion.Core.News.NewsArticleBank. Bodies under 'race.result|cause' compose fact tokens and {pool:name} references into 'pools'; champLine is appended to non-DNF bodies only. Allowed fact tokens: {player} {team} {race} {year} {round} {position} {expected} {winner} {fieldSize} {champPosition} {champMove} {champLeader}. Add eras/events by adding JSON only.

1980s: TRACKSIDE AT {race}, {year}. The wire's red-hot.
SPORTS DESK — {race}, round {round}.
LIVE FROM {race}: cameras rolling, boost climbing.
FROM THE PIT WALL, {race}, {year}.
PADDOCK REPORT — {race}, and the turbos are screaming.
Crank the boost and let them chase — this one's in the book.
The sponsors will love the column inches. Cue the champagne.
Turn it up: nobody's touching {team} in this mood.
One for the highlight reel, and there'll be plenty more.
The TV gantry got its money's worth this afternoon.
Kill the boost. The team wants this weekend forgotten.
Back to the drawing board, and fast.
The wick's turned down, and the mood in the garage with it.
No highlights, no headlines, just a long trip home.
The sponsors will want a word before the next round.
In the title race {player} sits {champPosition}, {champMove}.
The championship table now reads {player} {champPosition} — {champMove} on the year.
{player} climbs to {champPosition} in the points, {champMove}.
That's {player} {champPosition} in the standings, {champMove}.
default: That leaves {player} {champPosition} in the title race.

1980s: from a car the form guide had written off for {expected}
against a grid the pundits reckoned good only for {expected}
when the paperwork had pencilled in {expected} at best
The turbo did the talking.
A thousand horsepower, pointed straight.
Boost to the limit and no apologies.
The wick was wound up all afternoon.
Full song, full commitment from the turbo.
Millions watched it live.
Prime-time, coast to coast.
The cameras drank it in.
The highlight reel writes itself.
{pool:dateline} {player} WINS {race}! The {team} car led {fieldSize} rivals a merry dance and never once looked like losing. {pool:mediaLine} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} Lights to flag, {player} owned the {year} {race}. {team} wound up the boost, checked out, and left the rest scrapping for the scenery. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} VICTORY for {player} at {race} — stop the presses. {team} were untouchable, {pool:expectationBeat} and the form book torn up. {pool:boostLine} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
default: {player} won {race} for {team}, leading home a field of {fieldSize}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

1980s: {pool:dateline} Podium for {player} — {position} at {race}, splitting a field of {fieldSize}. Only {winner} finished ahead. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {player} muscled the {team} machine onto the box, home {position} at {race} with champagne all round. {pool:boostLine} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} ROSTRUM! {player} banks {position} at {race}, chasing {winner} home in front of the cameras. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} {player} bagged the points at {race}, home {position} from {fieldSize} starters. {winner} took the win, but the {team} account keeps ticking over. {pool:mediaLine} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} No podium, no drama — {player} pocketed a tidy {position} at {race}. Points banked, sponsors kept sweet. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} GIANT-KILLER! {player} hauled the {team} car up to {position} at {race} {pool:expectationBeat}. The paddock is still rubbing its eyes. {pool:boostLine} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {player} stole the show at {race}, {position} from a field of {fieldSize} and the drive everyone will be talking about on Monday. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} Off the pace and out of sorts: {player} could manage only {position} at {race} when {expected} was the least {team} wanted. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} A flat afternoon for {player}, home {position} in a field of {fieldSize} while {winner} ran away up front. {team} will be hunting answers by Monday. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}
default: {player} was below par at {race}, only {position} when {expected} was expected. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}

1980s: {pool:dateline} BLOWN! The {team} turbo let go at {race} and took {player}'s afternoon with it — no fault of the driver, just a machine shoved past breaking point, {expected} up in smoke. {winner} cashed in. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} Smoke, then silence. {player} was running hard at {race} when the {team} engine cried enough and {expected} vanished in an instant. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} INTO THE WALL! {player} binned the {team} car at {race} — a moment's greed on the boost and it was all over. {winner} led home {fieldSize} survivors. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} A costly mistake: {player} threw {race} away, off and out where {expected} had been there for the taking. {pool:closeBad}
default: {player} crashed out of {race} through driver error. {pool:closeBad}

1980s: {pool:dateline} A quiet shift for {player}, {position} at {race} from {fieldSize} starters. Nothing for the highlight reel, but the {team} car saw the flag. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {player} slotted home {position} at {race}, well adrift of {winner}. Mid-pack anonymity for {team} this time out. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
default: {player} ran to {position} at {race}, a quiet afternoon for {team}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
